item.title: Trump Deals Set To Fall Short of Sweeping Trade Reforms | Bloomberg: The Asia Trade 6/30/25
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
"Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets.
Chapters: 00:00:00 - Bloomberg: The Asia Trade begins 00:02:55 - Trump deals poised to fall short of sweeping global trade reforms 00:07:45 - Oxford Economics' Louise Loo on tariff talks 00:14:17 - Trump tax bill hits Senate with GOP torn by competing demands 00:19:09 - US Energy Secretary Chris Wright on a peace deal with Iran 00:24:36 - Australia cuts commodity export outlook despite gold price gain 00:28:23 - Key risks for stocks in 2H 2025 00:34:50 - Medipal Senior Managing Director Toshihide Yoda on business outlook 00:51:45 - Breaking: Japan's factory output misses expectations as US tariffs hit 00:54:23 - Masayoshi Son hints at succession plan while chasing AI ambition 00:57:22 - Markets open in Australia, South Korea & Japan 01:00:38 - UBS Global Wealth Management's Hartmut Issel on market strategy 01:08:09 - Trump says he doesn't expect to extend tariff deadline 01:22:25 - China June manufacturing PMIs expected to rise on trade truce with US 01:25:28 - Money managers cautious about the stock market in the second half 01:33:05 - Thai Prime Minister’s support slumps in poll amid protest 01:40:04 - Exclusive: Noetix Robotics orders race ahead after Beijing marathon -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets
Like this video? Subscribe and turn on notifications so you don't miss any videos from Bloomberg Markets & Finance: https://tinyurl.com/ysu5b8a9 Visit http://www.bloomberg.com for business news & analysis, up-to-the-minute market data, features, profiles and more.

item.title: Idaho police under sniper attack as ambush on firefighters leaves two dead
item.content:encoded: Governor Brad Little says "multiple heroic firefighters" were targeted near Coeur d'Alene.

item.title: Bridge returned error 401! (20269)
item.author: RSS-Bridge
item.content:encoded: https://www.reuters.com/pf/api/v3/content/fetch/articles-by-section-alias-or-id-v1?query={"section_id":"\/home","size":30,"website":"reuters"} resulted in 401 Unauthorized #0 index.php(72): RssBridge->main() #1 lib/RssBridge.php(103): DisplayAction->execute() #2 actions/DisplayAction.php(68): DisplayAction->createResponse() #3 actions/DisplayAction.php(117): ReutersBridge->collectData() #4 bridges/ReutersBridge.php(500): getContents() #5 lib/contents.php(110): HttpException::fromResponse() action=display&bridge=ReutersBridge&feed=home/topnews&format=Atom 2024-02-02 (git.master.257799b) Go back

item.title: English Football Association bans transgender women from women's soccer | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Since the beginning of this month, transgender women have been banned from playing women's football in England. Trans women had been allowed to participate in women's official games for years, though they were required to limit their testosterone levels. But after a Supreme court ruling on the definition of women, the English Football Association moved to ban trans women.
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews
Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1 #England #Transgender #Football #Soccer

item.title: Boeing's 787 Dreamliner was deemed the 'safest' of planes. The whistleblowers were always less sure
item.content:encoded: Long before the Air India tragedy, the cause of which is still to be determined, people who had worked on the 787 had raised concerns about the production standards

item.title: 'Every word has come back to haunt me': China cracks down on women who write gay erotica
item.content:encoded: "Every word I once wrote has come back to haunt me," posted a writer who says she was arrested for "obscene" work.

item.title: US must rule out more strikes before new talks, Iranian minister tells BBC
item.content:encoded: Majid Takht-Ravanchi said the Trump administration has "not made their position clear" on further attacks.

item.title: Nearly 12 million estimated to lose health coverage under Trump budget bill
item.content:encoded: The spending plan could also add nearly $3.3tn (£2.4tn) in debt, according to non-partisan analysis.

item.title: Crowds celebrate Pride in New York City, India, Mexico and more
item.content:encoded: Crowds have gathered at Pride celebrations around the world, including the Pride Parade NYC.

item.title: What options does Washington have to prevent Iran's nuclear advancement? | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
The head of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) says Iran could resume enriching uranium within months after the latest Israeli and US attacks on its nuclear facilities.
Rafael Grossi's statement contradicted President Donald Trump's claims that last week's bombing raids had obliterated Iran's nuclear program. The Iranian parliament has suspended its cooperation with the International Atomic Energy Agency following the strikes.
Rosemary Kelanic is the director of the Middle East program at Defense Priorities - a US-based think tank. Tehran says it has no plans to resume talks with the US. What options does Washington have now to prevent Iran's nuclear advancement?
00:00 MidEast: How can military proliferation be prevented? 00:44 Rosemary Kelanic, Defense Priorities think tank
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews
Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1 #Iran #Nuclear

item.title: 'Mariupol is diseased': Residents deny Russia's stories about occupied city
item.content:encoded: Russian shattered Mariupol with a months-long siege in 2022. Now, it says it's rebuilding it.

item.title: 'Mariupol is diseased': Residents deny Russia's stories about occupied city
item.content:encoded: Russian shattered Mariupol with a months-long siege in 2022. Now, it says it's rebuilding it.

item.title: Israel intensifies strikes on Gaza City after ordering people to evacuate to the south | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Israel's armed forces have intensified their strikes on Gaza City with dozens of people injured, according to Palestinian media reports. Earlier, Israel issued evacuation orders to people in the area, telling them to head south to Al-Mawasi in Rafah. The Israeli military says it's aiming to destroy the capabilities of terrorist organizations. The new evacuation order follows intense airstrikes this weekend, with Gaza's civil defense agency reporting that Israeli strikes and gunfire killed at least 23 people on Sunday, including several women and children.
0:00 DW speaks to two aid providers in Gaza 0:38 Amjad Shawa, Head of Palestinian NGO Network 6:14 Eyad Amawi, Gaza Relief Committee
#gaza #israel #middleeast
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews
Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1

item.title: Spain records temperature of 46C as Europe heatwave continues
item.content:encoded: Health warnings have been issued across many parts of Europe as temperatures soar to the mid-40s Celsius.

item.title: Trump says he has 'a group of very wealthy people' to buy TikTok
item.content:encoded: Trump says he thinks China's president will probably approve a sale to the unidentified group.

item.title: One of Hong Kong's last major pro-democracy parties disbands
item.content:encoded: Is it the third major opposition party to disband this year, ahead of the fifth anniversary of the Beijing-imposed national security law.

item.title: One of Hong Kong's last major pro-democracy parties disbands
item.content:encoded: It is the third major opposition party to disband this year, ahead of the fifth anniversary of the Beijing-imposed national security law.

item.title: Israel orders evacuations in northern Gaza as Trump pushes for ceasefire
item.content:encoded: At least 86 people were killed as the result of Israeli attacks in the 24 hours before midday Sunday, the Hamas-run health ministry said.

item.title: Diplomatic efforts are underway trying to convince Syria to join the Abraham Accords | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Israel has conducted military operations across the border with its neighbor Syria, following the departure of long-time ruler Bashar al-Assad late last year. Israel's army has released a video of, what it says, are its forces conducting a recent ground operation in Syria. The IDF says it was targeting terrorist infrastructure linked to hostile activities near its border, raiding a weapons storage site, and arresting several suspects. At the same time, there are diplomatic efforts -- including by US President Donald Trump -- to push Syria to join the US-brokered Abraham Accords which have normalized relations between several Arab states and Israel.
0:00 Syria didn’t publicly comment on Israel’s srikes on Iran 3:28 Nimrod Goren, President of Mitvim, The Israeli Institute for Regional Foreign Policies
#israel #syria #abrahamaccords
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Thousands in Norway told they won up to millions in lottery error
item.content:encoded: Norsk Tipping's CEO resigned a day after thousands received incorrect notifications, some saying they had become millionaires.

item.title: Thousands in Norway told they won up to millions in lottery error
item.content:encoded: Norsk Tipping's CEO resigned a day after thousands received incorrect notifications, some saying they had become millionaires.

item.title: US, Qatar push for ceasefire even as Israel intensifies strikes on Gaza | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
The US and Qatar say there is momentum towards a ceasefire deal, even as Israel has just announced it will intensify military action in Gaza. Can both of these things be true at the same time? Meanwhile, Israels strikes on Iran have pushed international condemnation of the war in Gaza to the background. Could that have been Prime Minister Benjamin Yetanyahu's aim?
DW speaks with Mairav Zonszein, Senior Analyst on Israel-Palestine for the International Crisis Group.
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews
Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1
#Gaza #Israel #Ceasefire

item.title: Can Socialism Work for Business in NYC?
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
After this week’s democratic primary election for New York City mayor, Wall Street has more questions than answers. Partnership for New York’s President and CEO Kathryn Wylde says the disconnect between Wall Street and Zohran Mamdani’s supporters explains the attractiveness of progressive policies in a city that symbolizes capitalism. Harvard Economist Ed Glaeser has studied the success and failure of democratic socialist ideals in cities across the United States and warns that New York is facing a "maximally risky moment." -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets
Like this video? Subscribe and turn on notifications so you don't miss any videos from Bloomberg Markets & Finance: https://tinyurl.com/ysu5b8a9 Visit http://www.bloomberg.com for business news & analysis, up-to-the-minute market data, features, profiles and more.

item.title: From Poker to the Trading Floor
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
From the poker table to the trading floor: A former pro player-turned-hedge fund founder says the skills overlap and tells Sonali Basak how.
Watch the full episode of Bullish on the Bloomberg app https://bloom.bg/4n4Ih9l
-------- Like this video? Subscribe: http://www.youtube.com/Bloomberg?sub_confirmation=1

Hosted by experts covering stories you haven’t seen and viewpoints you haven’t heard, you’ll discover cinematic, data-led shows that investigate the intersection of business and culture. item.title: The Price of Trump's Gold Card and Where Else You Can Live
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
The US has had a version of an investment visa since the early 20th century, so what’s new about President Trump’s "gold card" and how does it compare with other countries’ investment immigration programs? LSE’s Kristin Surak explains different incentives of countries' golden visa and passport programs and why some countries have shut them down. New Zealand’s Former Minister of Economic Development Stuart Nash explains country’s revamped investment immigration program. Cato Institute’s Alex Nowrasteh takes a different approach to immigration and says that the market is best placed to determine the right price of immigration. -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets
Like this video? Subscribe and turn on notifications so you don't miss any videos from Bloomberg Markets & Finance: https://tinyurl.com/ysu5b8a9 Visit http://www.bloomberg.com for business news & analysis, up-to-the-minute market data, features, profiles and more.

item.title: Why extortion and 'protection' racketeering are surging in South Africa | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
According to the Global Organized Crime Index, South Africa is sixth-worst in the world when it comes to extortion and protection racketeering. South African businesses are battling an alarming increase and the construction industry is hard hit by organized gangs demanding large sums of money - sometimes as much as 30 percent of project costs, which they claim is 'for protection.' But companies are being 'protected' by the very same gangs who are extorting them. DW’s Dianne Hawker has been following the story.
00:00 Extortion crimes soar in South Africa 04:03 DW speaks with Janine Erasmus, Website editor at Corruption Watch
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews
Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1
#SouthAfrica #Extortion #ProtectionRacketeering

item.title: Iran could start enriching uranium for bomb within months, UN nuclear chief says
item.content:encoded: Rafael Grossi's claim contradicts President Trump, who insists Iran's nuclear capabilities have been "totally obliterated".

item.title: Israel orders evacuation of northern Gaza as deaths continue to mount | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Israel's military has ordered Palestinians to evacuate from parts of northern Gaza, warning of intense and imminent action. Posting in Arabic on X, a spokesperson for the IDF said the military would operate with intense force in Jabaliya and Gaza City, and said people should evacuate south, to Al-Mawasi, for their own safety. The IDF says it is aiming to destroy terrorist organizations. The new evacuation order follows intense airstrikes this weekend. Citing local medical workers and the civil defence force, the DPA news agency reported that Israeli strikes have killed at least 56 people across Gaza since early Saturday. There are reports that children are amongst the dead.
00:00 Israel orders evacuation of northern Gaza 02:58 DW speaks with Shaina Low, spokesperson for the Norwegian Refugee Council
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews
Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1
#Gaza #Israel #Evacuation

item.title: Finding the perfect wave in the desert
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded: Wave pools were once considered the antithesis of surf culture. Now they're becoming increasingly popular, offering a controlled and accessible environment for surfers of all levels. Sunny Kim explains

item.title: Police clash with anti-government protesters demanding new elections | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Protesters have marched in the Serbian capital Belgrade against the government of President Aleksandar Vucic. Saturday's protests were the latest in months of anti-government demonstrations which have mostly been led by students. They began when part of a roof collapsed at a newly renovated railway station last year, resulting in 16 deaths and accusations of corruption. The protests ended with police firing tear gas after flares were thrown. DW's Idro Seferi reports from Belgrade.
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews
Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1
#Serbia #Belgrade #Protest

item.title: Thousands protest in Bangkok calling for Thai PM to resign
item.content:encoded: Demands to resign follow a leaked phone call the prime minister had with Cambodia's former leader about a border dispute.

item.title: Thousands protest in Bangkok calling for Thai PM to resign
item.content:encoded: Demands to resign follow a leaked phone call the prime minister had with Cambodia's former leader about a border dispute.

item.title: Queen of Katwe's gambit still in play for Uganda's slum chess players
item.content:encoded: A famous chess club is still producing champions, but faces a daily struggle to survive.

item.title: Iran could start enriching uranium for bomb within months, UN nuclear chief says
item.content:encoded: Rafael Grossi's claim contradicts President Trump, who insists Iran's nuclear capabilities have been "totally obliterated".

item.title: At least 81 people killed in Israeli strikes in Gaza, Hamas-run health ministry says
item.content:encoded: Hospital staff and witnesses said at least 11 people were killed after a strike near tents housing displaced people on Friday.

item.title: Kenya protests turn deadly again one year after crackdown | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Why is history repeating itself in Kenya? One year ago, thousands took to the streets of Nairobi protesting agains new tax laws, government corruption and police brutality. Sixty people died. A year later, nothing much has changed. The streets of the Kenyan capital again filled with demonstrators, teargas - and gunshots. Amnesty International says sixteen more people died on Wednesday, mostly killed by the police. But each civilian life lost, is strengthening the call among Kenyans for justice, as DW correspondent Sella Oneko reports from Nairobi.
00:00 New protests in Kenya turn deadly 05:26 DW's Edith Kimani reports from Nairobi
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews
Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1
#Kenya #Protests #PoliceBrutality

item.title: Mo Ibrahim on Financing in Africa, Telecoms, Sudan War
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
Businessman and philanthropist Mo Ibrahim gives his take on rethinking the financing framework across the continent, outlining why he thinks good governance is crucial for attracting investments. He also take about the state of telecoms in Africa after making his multi-billion dollar exit from the sector nearly two decades ago. Ibrahim also discusses the urgent need for a solution to the conflict in his country of origin, Sudan. He speaks to Bloomberg's Jennifer Zabasajja. -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

item.title: Elderly German drivers take a class in road safety for seniors | Focus on Europe
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
There’s no mandatory health check for older drivers in Germany. But when they’re involved in an accident, they’re the main cause in two out of three cases.
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Senate Republicans advance Trump's 'Big Beautiful Bill' but final vote hangs in balance
item.content:encoded: Senate leadership twisted arms for an initial vote on the spending plan, which still needs final approval.

item.title: Senate Republicans advance Trump's 'Big Beautiful Bill' but final vote hangs in balance
item.content:encoded: Senate leadership twisted arms for an initial vote on the spending plan, which still needs final approval.
